items.title: Jensen Huang Fires Back At Wall Street 'Flatlining' Nvidia Forecasts, Says AI Demand Will Crush Long-Term Expectations
items.description: Chipmaker Nvidia Corp.’s (NASDAQ: NVDA) CEO Jensen Huang pushed back against Wall Street's long-term outlook for the company, saying analysts are underestimating the scale of artificial intelligence demand. Dismisses Wall Street Forecasts On last ...

items.title: Stocks to Watch Recap: Electronic Arts, Lockheed Martin, Carnival, Alibaba
items.description: ↗️ Electronic Arts (EA): The videogame maker is going private for $52.5 billion in what it billed as the largest-ever private-equity backed buyout. Shares rose 4.5% Monday, while other video-game stocks such as Roblox (RBLX) and Take-Two Interactive Software (TTWO) also moved higher on the day.

items.title: Why EA Is Ready to Quit Wall Street’s Game
items.description: The videogame company is going private in a $55 billion deal, as it finally has a potential hit combat game on its hands.

items.title: DeepSeek: Everything you need to know about the AI chatbot app
items.description: DeepSeek is backed by High-Flyer Capital Management, a Chinese quantitative hedge fund that uses AI to inform its trading decisions.
